From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [The Day of the Beast (film)](/w/index.php?title=The_Day_of_the_Beast_(film)&redirect=no "The Day of the Beast (film)"))

This article is about the 1995 film. For other uses, see [The Day of the Beast (disambiguation)](/wiki/The_Day_of_the_Beast_(disambiguation) "The Day of the Beast (disambiguation)").

1995 film

| The Day of the Beast | |
| --- | --- |
| Theatrical release poster by [Oscar Mariné](/wiki/Oscar_Marin%C3%A9 "Oscar Mariné") | |
|  | |
| Spanish | *El día de la bestia* |
|  | |
| Directed by | [Álex de la Iglesia](/wiki/%C3%81lex_de_la_Iglesia "Álex de la Iglesia") |
| Written by | [Jorge Guerricaechevarría](/wiki/Jorge_Guerricaechevarr%C3%ADa "Jorge Guerricaechevarría") Álex de la Iglesia |
| Produced by | [Andrés Vicente Gómez](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9s_Vicente_G%C3%B3mez "Andrés Vicente Gómez") |
| Starring | * [Álex Angulo](/wiki/%C3%81lex_Angulo "Álex Angulo") * Armando De Razza * [Santiago Segura](/wiki/Santiago_Segura "Santiago Segura") * [Terele Pávez](/wiki/Terele_P%C3%A1vez "Terele Pávez") * [Nathalie Seseña](/wiki/Nathalie_Sese%C3%B1a "Nathalie Seseña") * [Maria Grazia Cucinotta](/wiki/Maria_Grazia_Cucinotta "Maria Grazia Cucinotta") * Saturnino García |
| Cinematography | [Flavio Martínez Labiano](/wiki/Flavio_Mart%C3%ADnez_Labiano "Flavio Martínez Labiano") |

| Release date | * 20 October 1995 (1995-10-20) (Spain) |

| Countries | * Spain * Italy |
| Language | Spanish |
| Budget | 300 million [₧](/wiki/Spanish_peseta "Spanish peseta") |
| Box office | €4,367,321 |

***The Day of the Beast*** (Spanish: ***El día de la bestia***) is a 1995 [black comedy](/wiki/Black_comedy "Black comedy")[[n. 1]](#cite_note-2) film co-written and directed by [Álex de la Iglesia](/wiki/%C3%81lex_de_la_Iglesia "Álex de la Iglesia") and starring [Álex Angulo](/wiki/%C3%81lex_Angulo "Álex Angulo"), Armando De Razza and [Santiago Segura](/wiki/Santiago_Segura "Santiago Segura"). It is a Spanish-Italian co-production.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBuseTriana_ToribioWillis200775,_182–183-3)

The plot concerns the unorthodox attempts of a [Basque](/wiki/Basques "Basques") priest (Angulo) to avert the birth of the [Antichrist](/wiki/Antichrist "Antichrist") in Madrid during [Christmas Eve](/wiki/Christmas_Eve "Christmas Eve"), teaming up with a metalhead (Segura) and an occult fraudster (De Razza). The film, marketed as "a satanic comedy",[[3]](#cite_note-4) was well received by critics and audiences in Spain, and sparked interest in the director's filmography and style of directing.

It earned numerous accolades, including the [Goya Award for Best Director](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Director "Goya Award for Best Director") for Álex de la Iglesia.

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Day_of_the_Beast&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

Ángel Berriatúa, a Basque priest and professor of theology, confesses to another priest that he intends to commit as much evil as he can. The other priest is shocked until Ángel whispers his reasoning. When the other priest agrees to help him, a large cross falls and crushes him.

Ángel goes into downtown Madrid, committing various sins and crimes such as taking money from a homeless man, pushing a street performer over, and telling a dying man to go to Hell and stealing his wallet. He goes to a record store and meets José María, a self-described Satanist and [heavy metal fan](/wiki/Heavy_metal_subculture "Heavy metal subculture") from [Carabanchel](/wiki/Carabanchel "Carabanchel"), who gives him a tape of the most "evil" band he can think of. José María puts Ángel up in his mother's boarding house where he lives with his mother, grandfather, and Mina, a woman he is attracted to who helps his mother with the business. Ángel then attempts to steal a book written by occult TV show host Professor Cavan, convinced it can tell him how to [sell his soul](/wiki/Deal_with_the_Devil "Deal with the Devil") to Satan, believing that a secret code in the Bible says that the [Antichrist](/wiki/Antichrist "Antichrist") will be born at midnight on [Christmas Eve](/wiki/Christmas_Eve "Christmas Eve"). He intends to sell his soul before the night is out to get into the birth ceremony and kill the Antichrist. The store manager does not believe him and he flees.

[![The corner of a building with neon signs advertising Capitol, Vodafone and Schweppes.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6f/Madrid_%2835020011746%29.jpg/250px-Madrid_%2835020011746%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Madrid_(35020011746).jpg)

The neon [Schweppes](/wiki/Schweppes "Schweppes") sign where José María hangs from is on the [Edificio Carrión](/wiki/Edificio_Carri%C3%B3n "Edificio Carrión").[[4]](#cite_note-Madrid-5)

Ángel and José María team up to kidnap and tie up Cavan, who they intimidate into telling them how to complete the ritual, which requires the blood of a [virgin](/wiki/Virginity "Virginity"). Ángel first tries to get it from Cavan's girlfriend Susana, but after knocking her out by mistake, Cavan admits that she is not a virgin. Ángel then goes back to the boarding house to try with Mina but José María's mother sees him and tries to kill him with a shotgun. Ángel escapes with some of Mina's blood, but accidentally kills José María's mother. Ángel, José María, and Cavan are able to burn a piece of paper, take LSD, and complete the ritual, which Cavan believes is made up. However, a [he-goat](/wiki/Baphomet "Baphomet") appears in the room and then disappears. Ángel then finds a message in the burnt scraps of paper in which the devil taunts him, not fooled by Ángel's actions. Ángel, José María, and Cavan flee the apartment when the police arrive on suspicion of Cavan's kidnapping.

[![A crowded street with decorative lights.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/da/Calle_Preciados%2C_Madrid.jpg/250px-Calle_Preciados%2C_Madrid.jpg)](/wiki/File:Calle_Preciados,_Madrid.jpg)

De la Iglesia stages a shooting in [Calle Preciados](/wiki/Calle_Preciados "Calle Preciados"), a commercial street in Madrid, specially crowded during Christmas.[[4]](#cite_note-Madrid-5)

Ángel and José María frantically drive around town looking for a sign to point Ángel in the right direction. Ángel first tries to get the information on the birth of the Antichrist out of a man giving a speech about the predictions of [Nostradamus](/wiki/Nostradamus "Nostradamus"), resulting in a chase in which three men dressed as the [Three Wise Men](/wiki/Three_Wise_Men "Three Wise Men") are shot by police by mistake. He then tries to find the information at a heavy metal show, but is beaten up by the attendees. Cavan goes on television and asks Ángel to call him, telling him that he's realized Ángel was right and he knows where the birth of the Antichrist will be.

[![A stone monument stands in the center. Two skyscrapers leans to the center. The sky has been ominously altered.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/77/Puertadeeuropa.jpg/250px-Puertadeeuropa.jpg)](/wiki/File:Puertadeeuropa.jpg)

The Gate of Europe are the inclined buildings where the final fight happens.[[4]](#cite_note-Madrid-5)

Cavan points out that the devil uses [his own markings](/wiki/Sigil "Sigil"), similar yet opposite to the Christian cross, in order to mock God, and points them toward a pair of buildings in the same shape of the devil's mark: the [Gate of Europe](/wiki/Gate_of_Europe "Gate of Europe"). The three men engage in a fight with an extreme-right gang that has holed up there and has been seen throughout the film murdering homeless people and spray painting *Limpia Madrid* (transl. clean up Madrid) everywhere. Their leader is revealed as the devil when he throws José María off the building to his death. The gang beats Cavan badly, but Ángel gets hold of a gun and kills the gang, which was also killing a baby who is the Antichrist. He then shoots the devil as well.

Some time later, another actor takes over Cavan's show, and Ángel and Cavan become homeless drifters; while Cavan constantly complains that they will never be able to tell anyone how they saved the world, Ángel simply misses José María, but has accepted the events as the duo's fate.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Day_of_the_Beast&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Álex Angulo](/wiki/%C3%81lex_Angulo "Álex Angulo") as Father Ángel Berriartúa
* [Armando De Razza](/w/index.php?title=Armando_De_Razza&action=edit&redlink=1 "Armando De Razza (page does not exist)") [[it](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Armando_De_Razza "it:Armando De Razza")] as Professor Cavan
* [Santiago Segura](/wiki/Santiago_Segura "Santiago Segura") as José María
* [Terele Pávez](/wiki/Terele_P%C3%A1vez "Terele Pávez") as Rosario
* [Nathalie Seseña](/wiki/Nathalie_Sese%C3%B1a "Nathalie Seseña") as Mina
* [Maria Grazia Cucinotta](/wiki/Maria_Grazia_Cucinotta "Maria Grazia Cucinotta") as Susana
* [Jaime Blanch](/wiki/Jaime_Blanch "Jaime Blanch")
* [Antonio Dechent](/wiki/Antonio_Dechent "Antonio Dechent")
* [Saturnino García](/w/index.php?title=Saturnino_Garc%C3%ADa&action=edit&redlink=1 "Saturnino García (page does not exist)") [[es](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturnino_Garc%C3%ADa "es:Saturnino García")]
* [Ramón Agirre](/w/index.php?title=Ram%C3%B3n_Agirre_Lasarte&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ramón Agirre Lasarte (page does not exist)") [[es](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ram%C3%B3n_Agirre_Lasarte "es:Ramón Agirre Lasarte")]
* [El Gran Wyoming](/wiki/El_Gran_Wyoming "El Gran Wyoming")
* [Jimmy Barnatán](/wiki/Jimmy_Barnat%C3%A1n "Jimmy Barnatán"), as *possessed child*

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Day_of_the_Beast&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

A Spanish-Italian 80%–20% co-production,[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBuseTriana_ToribioWillis2007182-6) the film was produced by [Andrés Vicente Gómez](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9s_Vicente_G%C3%B3mez "Andrés Vicente Gómez")' Iberoamericana Films Producción alongside Sogetel and M.G. S.R.L in collaboration with Sogepaq and [Canal Plus España](/wiki/Canal%2B_(Spanish_TV_channel) "Canal+ (Spanish TV channel)").[[6]](#cite_note-7) It had a 300 million [₧](/wiki/Spanish_peseta "Spanish peseta") budget.[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBuseTriana_ToribioWillis2007183-8) A soundtrack for the film was released in Spain and Korea. It featured bands such as [Ministry](/wiki/Ministry_(band) "Ministry (band)"), [HeadCrash](/wiki/HeadCrash_(band) "HeadCrash (band)"), [Pantera](/wiki/Pantera "Pantera"), [Sugar Ray](/wiki/Sugar_Ray "Sugar Ray"), and [Def Con Dos](/wiki/Def_Con_Dos "Def Con Dos").[[8]](#cite_note-9)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Day_of_the_Beast&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Release")]

The film opened in Spanish theatres on 20 October 1995.[[9]](#cite_note-10) It grossed [€](/wiki/Euro "Euro")4,367,321.[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBuseTriana_ToribioWillis2007183-8)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Day_of_the_Beast&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Reception")]

In a retrospective review of the film for Popoptiq, reviewer Ricky D. wrote, "Delirious, demented and diabolically funny, *The Day of the Beast* is essential viewing."[[10]](#cite_note-11)

In a retrospective review for Daily Grindhouse, reviewer Johnny Donaldson wrote that *The Day of the Beast* "may not exactly be a traditional Christmas movie, even by the standards of the horror genre, but it's a perfect one for those who want to thumb their noses at the 'Christ' part of Christmas."[[11]](#cite_note-12)

## Accolades

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Day_of_the_Beast&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Accolades")]

| Year | Award | Category | Nominee(s) | Result | Ref. |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1996 | [10th Goya Awards](/wiki/10th_Goya_Awards "10th Goya Awards") | [Best Film](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Film "Goya Award for Best Film") | Sogetel, Iberoamericana Films, M.G. S.R.L. | Nominated | [[12]](#cite_note-13) |
| [Best Director](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Director "Goya Award for Best Director") | Álex de la Iglesia | Won |
| [Best Actor](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Actor "Goya Award for Best Actor") | Álex Angulo | Nominated |
| [Best Original Screenplay](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Original_Screenplay "Goya Award for Best Original Screenplay") | Jorge Guerricaechevarría, Álex de la Iglesia | Nominated |
| [New Actor](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_New_Actor "Goya Award for Best New Actor") | Santiago Segura | Won |
| [Best Art Direction](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Art_Direction "Goya Award for Best Art Direction") | Arturo García "Biafra", José Luis Arrizabalaga | Won |
| [Best Cinematography](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Cinematography "Goya Award for Best Cinematography") | Flavio Martínez Labiano | Nominated |
| [Best Editing](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Editing "Goya Award for Best Editing") | Teresa Font | Nominated |
| [Best Production Supervision](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Production_Supervision "Goya Award for Best Production Supervision") | Carmen Martínez | Nominated |
